# Hanwha Vision Camera MCP Server

> This file provides context for LLMs interacting with the hanwha-mcp server.

## What this server does

This MCP server connects to Hanwha Vision (Wisenet) IP cameras and exposes their SUNAPI configuration interface as MCP tools. It enables reading and writing camera settings over HTTP.

## Connection workflow

You MUST call `connect_camera` with host, username, and password before using any other tool. All subsequent tools operate on the connected camera. Only one camera connection is active at a time — calling `connect_camera` again switches to the new camera.

## Tool categories

### Read-only tools (safe, no side effects)
- `get_device_info` — model, serial, firmware, MAC, video profiles
- `get_snapshot` — live JPEG image (default resized to 1280px wide)
- `get_image_settings` — brightness, sharpness, white balance, exposure, SSDR, flip, IR
- `get_overlay` — title/time overlay configuration
- `get_osd_list` — multiline on-screen display entries
- `get_datetime` — time, timezone, DST, NTP servers
- `get_network_info` — MAC, link status, DNS
- `get_logs` — system/access/event logs

### Write tools (modify camera settings, idempotent)
- `set_image_settings` — brightness (0-100), sharpness, gamma, saturation (0-100)
- `set_flip` — horizontalFlip (bool), verticalFlip (bool), rotate (Off/90/180/270)
- `set_ir_led` — mode (Auto/Off/On)
- `set_focus` — mode (SimpleAutoFocus/ManualFocus)
- `set_overlay` — title text, time format, font size, positions
- `set_osd` — add/update OSD entry by index (1-8) with type, text, color, size, position
- `set_ntp` — comma-separated NTP server list
- `set_datetime` — manual date/time (disables NTP)

### Destructive tools (use with caution)
- `remove_osd` — permanently deletes an OSD entry by index
- `reboot_camera` — reboots the camera (~60 second downtime)

## Parameter conventions

- `channel` defaults to 0 on all tools. Multi-channel cameras (e.g. multi-sensor) use channels 0, 1, 2, etc.
- Boolean values on the camera use the strings "True" and "False" but tool inputs accept native booleans.
- Position values (X/Y) range from 0-9 representing grid positions.
- Font sizes are: Small, Medium, Large.
- OSD colors: White, Red, Blue, Green, Yellow, Cyan, Magenta, Black.

## Common workflows

### Inspect a camera
1. `connect_camera` → 2. `get_device_info` → 3. `get_image_settings` → 4. `get_snapshot`

### Add a text overlay
1. `connect_camera` → 2. `set_overlay` with titleEnable=true, title="Building A - Lobby" → 3. `get_snapshot` to verify

### Add custom OSD text
1. `connect_camera` → 2. `set_osd` with index=1, enable=true, osdType="Text", text="RESTRICTED AREA", osdColor="Red", fontSize="Large" → 3. `get_snapshot` to verify

### Configure NTP
1. `connect_camera` → 2. `set_ntp` with servers="pool.ntp.org,time.nist.gov" → 3. `get_datetime` to confirm

## Error handling

All tools return structured errors via `isError: true` in the MCP response. Common errors:
- "No camera connected" — call `connect_camera` first
- "SUNAPI ... failed: 401" — wrong credentials
- "SUNAPI ... failed: 404" — camera doesn't support that feature
- "Camera returned error: NG..." — camera-side error with error code

## SUNAPI protocol notes

- Endpoint pattern: `http://{host}/stw-cgi/{module}.cgi?msubmenu={sub}&action={action}`
- Auth: HTTP Digest
- Responses: `text/plain` with `Key=Value` lines
- Snapshots: raw JPEG bytes from `video.cgi?msubmenu=snapshot&action=view`
- Not all cameras support all endpoints. The server returns errors gracefully for unsupported features.
